title: Share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age
caption:
Share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age
Percentage of children at lower secondary school age meeting reading comprehension standards. This data accounts for school completion rates to estimate reading skills for all children, not just those in school.

The bar chart ranks countries by the percentage of children at lower secondary school age meeting reading comprehension standards in 2022, with bars sorted from highest to lowest. The vertical axis measures the share of proficient children from 0% to 80%, while the color of each bar identifies the country's income classification: Low-income countries appear in dark purple, Lower-middle-income countries in light purple, Upper-middle-income countries in light green, and High-income countries in dark green. Ireland leads the distribution with the highest share, exceeding 80%, followed closely by Australia and Lithuania, which also sit near the top of the range. Spain and Turkey follow, with Turkey marking a transition point where the income classification shifts to Upper-middle-income. Greece, Moldova, and Brazil occupy the middle section of the chart, with values descending from roughly 60% down to the 40% mark. Kazakhstan and Azerbaijan continue the downward slope within the Upper-middle-income band. The Dominican Republic and Cambodia anchor the lower end of the distribution, with Cambodia recording the lowest visible share, falling below 20%. High-income countries dominate the upper half of the chart, while Lower-middle-income and Low-income countries cluster in the bottom quartile. The data shows a clear gradient where higher national income levels generally coincide with higher reading proficiency rates among children.
